name: task-ESE-0016
title: Add Storybook to project. One story per ticket, matching ticket nesting structure
description: Add Storybook to the project as the demo catalogue. Update the agile process so that each ticket's demo produces at least one Storybook story. Stories should mirror the ticket nesting structure (parent ticket = story group, subtasks = individual stories). This replaces the ad-hoc demo artifact capture with a persistent, browsable demo catalogue. We want storybook to replace the current fragile, error prone 'sprint' system. We will get rid of sprints and velocity entirely. Intead, each time we create a ticket, we'll create a top level story in storybook. Each subtask will get its own directory... which can have their own sub directories too. Tickets will live as json files in storybook stories. All stories will render the ticket as a story. Additionally, all demo assets (demo scripts, artifacts presented to context free agent, and context free agent evaluation) will live in that directory. We will get rid of sprints entirely. Instead of measuring velocity, we will just record devStartTime and doneTime, and use this to refine storypoint estimates going forward. Backlog tickets must be translated to stories. Existing archived tickets can be let in archive. agile tooling must be updated. Screenshot functionality should be made easily findable, and the components to build and run tests should be reusable and intuitive to stumble upon
acceptanceCriteria:
- Storybook installed and running (npm run storybook)
- Each ticket maps to a story directory: stories/<ticket-name>/
- Ticket JSON lives inside its story directory
- Demo scripts, artifacts, and agent evaluations live in the story directory
- Parent tickets render as story groups; subtasks as child stories
- Every story renders its ticket JSON as a readable story
- Screenshot capture tooling is a reusable Storybook component/utility
- Backlog tickets translated to stories (archived tickets can stay in archive)
- Status flow simplified to 4 states: refinement → dev → review → done
- Review gate enforced: agent code review + context-free demo validation + human acceptance
- Sprint system removed — no sprint directories, velocity tracking, or sprint commands
- Ticket tracks devStartTime and doneTime for estimation refinement
- Agile CLI updated: ticket create makes a story dir, status flow uses 4 states
- npm run storybook launches the full demo catalogue
demoDeliverable: Run npm run storybook. Show the story catalogue with all existing tickets rendered as stories. Navigate to feat-ESE-0012 (GPU compute) and show the parent story with 6 subtask stories nested beneath it. Open a subtask story — show the ticket JSON rendered and the demo artifacts (screenshots, console output). Run a screenshot capture from within Storybook. Show that creating a new ticket via the CLI automatically creates a story directory.
testingScenarios:
- npm run storybook launches without errors
- All existing tickets appear as stories
- Ticket nesting is correct (parent/subtask hierarchy)
- Ticket JSON renders readably in each story
- Demo artifacts (screenshots, console output) display in stories
- CLI ticket create produces a story directory
- CLI ticket status works without sprint references
- Sprint commands removed or deprecated
- devStartTime/doneTime recorded on tickets
- Screenshot utility is importable and documented
